name: Fillet of Fish Florentine
recipeIngredient: 21 oz Frozen spinach, cooked per package directions
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Make the spinach according to package directions, then drain in a colander, pressing with the back of a spoon to extract as much liquid as possible.
3. Place the fish fillets in a baking dish and bring the court bouillon to a simmer on the stove.
4. Pour the simmering liquid into the baking dish, cover with a sheet of buttered wax paper (buttered side down), and bake for 5 to 7 minutes.
5. Drain the cooking liquid and reserve it.
6. Melt the butter in a saucepan over low heat. Add the flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 3 minutes.
7. Whisk in the reserved cooking liquid and bring to a boil. Add the cream and cheese to the sauce, and stir until smooth and bubbly. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Preheat the oven broiler.
9. Stir half the sauce into the spinach and arrange the spinach on an ovenproof serving platter.
10. Arrange the fish on top of the spinach, then top each fillet with some of the sauce.
11. Sprinkle the Parmesan over the sauce and place under the broiler for 1 to 2 minutes, or until lightly browned.
12. Serves 6. If assembled a day in advance, refrigerate covered. Then, rather than broiling, heat the dish in a 350°F oven until hot, about 15 minutes.
